title: Plugin chain P2: repoRoot retargeting — operate on the user's project, not the plugin dir
description:
Second ticket of the plugin packaging chain (epic TASK-020). Per `tasks/TASK-020.research.md` §B: when installed, the plugin code lives in an immutable cache dir, but all project I/O must target the **user's** project root (`${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}`), never the plugin's own location. The audit (§B.2) found the blast radius is small — only the `bin/` CLI shells bind `process.cwd()`; every `src/` function already takes `repoRoot`.

Scope:
1. Bind the shipped `bin/` shells (`init.js`, `new-task.js`) to `process.env.CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR ?? process.cwd()`. The exported `runInit`/`runCli` are already fully `repoRoot`-parameterized — only the shell binding changes. (make-template excluded from ship per P1, but if it stays in the repo, apply the same fallback for dev use.)
2. **Empirically verify** (§B.3 caveat / §I Q1) whether `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` is actually set in the **Bash-tool subprocess** environment on this machine — the docs guarantee it for hook/MCP subprocesses but not verbatim for Bash-tool calls. If it is NOT set, implement the documented fallback: a `SessionStart` hook that derives/exports the project dir, or route init exclusively through the slash command. Record the empirical result.
3. Confirm the framework-asset reads (`src/task-store.js` → `../tasks/schema.json`; `src/project-md.js` → `../state/PROJECT.schema.json`, §B.2 #8/#9) still resolve INSIDE the plugin cache after the §A.4 layout (intra-plugin relative reads are preserved; only outside-plugin `../` traversal is blocked).
acceptance_criteria: `bin/init.js` and `bin/new-task.js` resolve `repoRoot` from `process.env.CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` with a `process.cwd()` fallback. Unit tests assert BOTH branches (env var present → uses it; absent → falls back to cwd).

The empirical question 'is CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR present in a Bash-tool subprocess?' is answered with a recorded result. If absent, a SessionStart-hook (or equivalent) fallback is implemented and tested so the project root is still discoverable.

Framework-asset reads (tasks/schema.json, state/PROJECT.schema.json) are confirmed resolvable from their src/ modules under the plugin-root layout — a test loads task-store.js + project-md.js and confirms schema resolution does not throw.
No regression: the full existing suite passes, and `runInit`/`runCli` still work when given an explicit `repoRoot` (the dev/test path).

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Minted from TASK-020 spike §F (P2) + §B. The meatiest design section, but the impl is small per the audit. AC2's empirical CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR check is the gating unknown — verify before assuming the fallback is unnecessary.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: EMPIRICAL RESULT (orchestrator sensor, pre-chain): `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` is UNSET in a Bash-tool subprocess on this machine/version (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, CLAUDE_PROJECT_NAME, CLAUDECODE et al. ARE set — but not _DIR). HOWEVER `pwd`/`process.cwd()` of a Bash-tool call DOES equal the project root. Implications for P2: (1) the `process.env.CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR ?? process.cwd()` binding is correct AND the cwd branch is the load-bearing one in practice — do not assume the env var exists; (2) the cwd fallback only holds if the invoking command's cwd is the project root (true for bin/ bare-command + `node` calls the Bash tool makes from the project root, but NOT if a command cd's elsewhere first); (3) the robust belt-and-suspenders is a SessionStart hook that EXPORTS CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (hooks reliably receive it per docs) so subprocesses inherit it regardless of cwd. Recommend implementing both the cwd fallback AND the SessionStart export. This downgrades AC2 from 'unknown' to 'known: env var absent, cwd works, add hook for robustness'.
